pattern: Maximizing the minimum edge cost under a budget is monotone in the threshold, so binary searching the bottleneck value and checking budget feasibility with a cheapest-path search on the surviving edges yields the answer.
hints: If a path with every edge at least x and total cost within budget exists, then it also exists for any smaller threshold, so the best achievable bottleneck can be binary searched.
For a fixed threshold, drop every edge cheaper than it and every offline intermediate node, then ask whether the cheapest remaining 0-to-(n-1) path fits the budget.
Search over the sorted distinct edge costs; the largest threshold whose minimum-cost path stays within k is the answer, or -1 if no budgeted path exists at all.

edge_inputs.description: an offline node forces the other route

stress.description: random small DAGs (edges go low index to high) with a generous budget, checked by path enumeration
